perturbation.original_text: "Product Price" means with respect to a pound of Terpene Product the Terpene Cost of that Terpene Product, plus the Margin. "Terpene Cost" means the cost per pound to FCC of the raw materials incorporated by FCC into Terpene Product, computed based on the methodology used by FCC to account for its inventory (e.g. LIFO, FIFO) (provided that such method is in accordance with Generally Accepted Accounting Principles, consistently applied), plus a deemed allocation of other manufacturing costs of FCC of $[***] per pound.
perturbation.changed_text: "Product Price" means with respect to a pound of Terpene Product the Terpene Cost of that Terpene Product, plus the Surcharge. "Terpene Cost" means the cost per pound to FCC of the raw materials incorporated by FCC into Terpene Product, computed based on the methodology used by FCC to account for its inventory (e.g. LIFO, FIFO) (provided that such method is in accordance with Generally Accepted Accounting Principles, consistently applied), plus a deemed allocation of other manufacturing costs of FCC of $[***] per pound.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Margin' has been replaced with 'Surcharge' in the definition of 'Product Price'. While they could be argued as similar, the contract doesn't define the term 'Surcharge', which creates uncertainty and ambiguity in later sections that refer to 'Margin' without clarifying if it is interchangeable with 'Surcharge'. This can cause confusion when calculating the 'Product Price'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Representatives of Flotek and FCC shall consult with each other from time to time during the Term to discuss and resolve any issues arising from the performance of this Agreement. The parties may, by written agreement, revise the Maximum Quantity, the Margin, the Target Margin, or the Product Price. Flotek and FCC may agree in writing from time to time that quantities of Terpene Product over the Maximum Quantity shall be purchased by Flotek pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Product Price' using 'Margin.' The changed text replaces 'Margin' with 'Surcharge,' a term not defined in the contract. The contradicted text references 'Margin' in the context of revising the 'Product Price.' Because 'Surcharge' is not defined, and the contradicted text explicitly uses the term 'Margin,' there is ambiguity as to whether the parties can revise the product price based on 'Surcharge'. This introduces a contradiction in that the agreement as to revisions to the Margin may no longer be clear or enforceable because 'Margin' may or may not now be interpreted to mean 'Surcharge.' This corresponds to the definition of misaligned terminology, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Shipments of Terpene Product to Flotek must be made by FCC from FCC's facility within three (3) days of the date of the applicable purchase order. Delivery terms shall be FOB Winter Haven, Florida. Transportation shall be arranged by Flotek. All sales of Terpene Product subject to this Agreement shall be pursuant to the terms and conditions attached hereto as Exhibit B.
perturbation.changed_text: Shipments of Terpene Product to Flotek must be made by FCC from FCC's facility within five (5) business days of the date of the applicable purchase order. Delivery terms shall be Ex Works Winter Haven, Florida. Transportation shall be arranged by Flotek. All sales of Terpene Product subject to this Agreement shall be pursuant to the terms and conditions attached hereto as Exhibit B.
perturbation.explanation: The delivery timeframe has been changed from 'three (3) days' to 'five (5) business days' which creates a difference in timeline for the shipments of Terpene Product. The delivery terms have been altered from 'FOB Winter Haven, Florida' to 'Ex Works Winter Haven, Florida.' These shipping terms are legally distinct, dictating different responsibilities for the seller (FCC) and the buyer (Flotek) regarding the transportation and risk of loss, there is a direct contradiction in the defined delivery timeline and shipping terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Flotek shall order Terpene Product by sending to FCC written purchase orders in the form attached hereto as Exhibit A. FCC shall promptly, but in no event later than three (3) days after the date of such purchase order, confirm its acceptance or rejection of such purchase order by written notice to Flotek. FCC shall be obligated to accept all such purchase orders unless the amount ordered for a Year exceeds the Maximum Quantity, or orders for a particular quarter exceed [***] pounds or exceed the Flotek forecast for that quarter by more than 25%, provided, however, that, in such event, FCC shall communicate Flotek of the time period that will be required to satisfy such order assuming FCC endeavors to satisfy such order as soon as practicable, and Flotek shall inform FCC within ten (10) days of such communication whether it will order such Terpene Product pursuant to the revised delivery terms.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the shipment timeframe from 'three (3) days' to 'five (5) business days' and the delivery terms from 'FOB Winter Haven, Florida' to 'Ex Works Winter Haven, Florida.' The contradicted text states that 'FCC shall promptly, but in no event later than three (3) days after the date of such purchase order, confirm its acceptance or rejection of such purchase order'. The change introduces a contradiction because the contradicted section indicates that FCC has three days to confirm the order. However, the altered text gives FCC five business days for the entire shipment, which creates an inconsistency with the three-day confirmation timeline and potentially conflicts with Flotek's ability to order Terpene Product within a specific time period as stated in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that Flotek does not order the Minimum Quantity in a given Year, FCC may ship to Flotek the remaining quantity of Terpene Product prior to the end of such Year, and invoice Flotek as described in Section 6(c).
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that Flotek does not order the Agreed Upon Quantity in a given Year, FCC may ship to Flotek the remaining quantity of Terpene Product prior to the end of such Year, and invoice Flotek as described in Section 6(c).
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Minimum Quantity' has been replaced by 'Agreed Upon Quantity'. This substitution introduces ambiguity because 'Agreed Upon Quantity' is not defined anywhere else in the contract, while 'Minimum Quantity' has a specific definition in Section 1. This creates confusion regarding the amount of Terpene Product Flotek is actually obligated to order.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes hereof: "Affiliate" means any party controlled by, controlling under common control with, the party to whom the reference is made. "Margin" means $[***] per pound of Terpene Product. "Maximum Quantity" means [***] pounds of Terpene Product per Year, prorated for any partial Year included in the Term. "Minimum Quantity" means [***] pounds of Terpene Product per Year, prorated for any partial Year included in the Term. "Product Price" means with respect to a pound of Terpene Product the Terpene Cost of that Terpene Product, plus the Margin. "Terpene Cost" means the cost per pound to FCC of the raw materials incorporated by FCC into Terpene Product, computed based on the methodology used by FCC to account for its inventory (e.g. LIFO, FIFO) (provided that such method is in accordance with Generally Accepted Accounting Principles, consistently applied), plus a deemed allocation of other manufacturing costs of FCC of $[***] per pound. "Terpene Product" means terpene from citrus with a minimum d-limonene content of 94%, with the specifications set forth in Exhibit A to this Agreement. "Year" means a calendar year.
perturbation.justification: The original text references 'Minimum Quantity,' which is specifically defined in the 'Contradicted Text' as '[***] pounds of Terpene Product per Year'. The changed text replaces 'Minimum Quantity' with 'Agreed Upon Quantity,' which is not defined anywhere in the contract. This creates a contradiction because the contract now refers to a quantity that has no clear definition, rendering the obligation unclear and unenforceable. This falls under the definition of Misaligned Terminology which results in an in-text contradiction.
